title: Clay Bodies 101
summary: Understand earthenware, stoneware, and porcelain, and when to choose each.
steps: Use earthenware for low-fire color work and quick cycles.
Use stoneware for durable functional ware at mid/high fire.
Use porcelain for fine detail and translucent results.
Track shrinkage and warping with simple test bars.

title: Essential Tools & Studio Setup
summary: Build a clean, safe, low-stress workspace from day one.
steps: Start with core tools: rib, needle, sponge, wire, trimming loop.
Create separate wet and dry zones to reduce dust exposure.
Label reclaim, slip, and test buckets clearly.
Use wet-cleaning methods instead of dry sweeping.

title: Handbuilding Foundations
summary: Master pinch, coil, and slab methods with strong joins and clean forms.
steps: Compress base surfaces to prevent S-cracks.
Score and slip all structural joints.
Work in stages and let forms firm up between steps.
Control thickness consistency using guides.

title: Wheel Throwing Basics
summary: Center confidently, pull even walls, and finish clean rims.
steps: Focus first on posture and bracing before speed.
Center with controlled pressure, not force.
Pull walls in 2-3 deliberate passes.
Compress the rim every pass to avoid cracking.

title: Trimming & Foot Design
summary: Improve balance, visual weight, and function through intentional footwork.
steps: Trim at true leather-hard for predictable cuts.
Design foot profiles to match form language.
Use chucks for narrow-necked forms and lidded sets.
Compare finished weights and adjust trim strategy.

title: Drying Control & Crack Prevention
summary: Use moisture management to reduce losses and improve consistency.
steps: Dry bottoms slowly by flipping onto boards early.
Cover rims/lips to even moisture loss.
Batch similar thicknesses together.
Use damp boxes for multi-day projects.

title: Surface Design & Decoration
summary: Layer slips, underglazes, carving, and texture in a controlled workflow.
steps: Map your decoration timing from wet to bisque stage.
Use test tiles for line quality and color bleed.
Keep motif scale proportional to form volume.
Document brush/load ratios for repeatability.

title: Glaze Application Strategies
summary: Control thickness and overlap so glaze results are intentional.
steps: Target a consistent specific gravity in buckets.
Track dip time and drain time for repeatable coats.
Wipe feet clean and test overlap combinations.
Label all tests with clay body and firing schedule.

title: Kiln Operation & Firing Curves
summary: Design firing schedules that fit clay, glaze, and atmosphere goals.
steps: Use witness cones on multiple kiln shelves.
Tune ramps/holds based on glaze maturity and defects.
Record every firing with load notes and outcomes.
Adjust one variable per cycle for clean comparisons.

title: Defect Diagnosis
summary: Systematically solve pinholing, crazing, crawling, and dunting.
steps: Classify defects by stage: drying, bisque, glaze, cooling.
Check glaze fit against clay expansion behavior.
Review application thickness and bisque temperature.
Build controlled A/B test batches before full runs.

title: Small-Batch Production Systems
summary: Create repeatable workflows for sets, market runs, and commissions.
steps: Standardize dimensions with simple jigs and calipers.
Use stage gates: throw, trim, glaze, final QA.
Track cycle time and reject reasons weekly.
Maintain backup clay/glaze batches for continuity.

title: Developing a Signature Style
summary: Evolve a coherent body of work with clear visual identity.
steps: Define shape language: silhouette, proportion, rhythm.
Limit palette and glaze system for cohesive collections.
Review work in grouped series, not isolated pieces.
Refine based on intent, not novelty.

title: Critique, Reflection, and Iteration
summary: Use structured critique loops to improve faster and deeper.
steps: Photograph each batch under consistent lighting.
Score forms on function, balance, and finish.
Write post-firing notes and next-cycle actions.
Plan your next tests directly from critique outcomes.

title: Professional Practice & Sustainability
summary: Scale your ceramics practice with healthy systems and long-term resilience.
steps: Set pricing from labor, overhead, and breakage realities.
Build inventory tiers: everyday, seasonal, collector.
Design packaging standards for shipping durability.
Incorporate reclaim, efficient firing, and low-waste habits.
